description:
You are acting as a deployment engineer who must prepare an application for the new “v1.4.0” release.  
    Everything must be done from the Linux terminal in the non-privileged account “user” (whose home is “/home/user”).

    PRE-EXISTING MATERIAL
    1. Database  
       • Path: /home/user/app/data/app.db  
       • Type: SQLite 3  
       • Current schema: one table called “users” (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT).  
    2. SQL migration script  
       • Path: /home/user/update_scripts/02_create_release_info.sql  
       • Exact content (4 lines, ending with a newline):  
         CREATE TABLE release_info(  
             id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,  
             version TEXT NOT NULL,  
             deployed_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP  
         );  
    3. Application logs that need archiving  
       • Directory: /home/user/app/logs  
       • Contains several “*.log” files.

    DESIRED END STATE
    1. A bit-for-bit backup of the pre-migration database must exist at  
       /home/user/backups/app_backup_pre_release.db
    2. The live database (/home/user/app/data/app.db) must contain a new table named “release_info” **and** exactly one row whose “version” value is “v1.4.0”.  
       The rest of the original data (e.g., the “users” table and its rows) must remain intact.
    3. Every file that is currently inside /home/user/app/logs must be archived into  
       /home/user/app/logs_archive_pre_release.tar.gz  
       The archive must be a standard gzip-compressed “ustar” (tar + gzip) file whose root directory entries are exactly the original “*.log” files—no extra directory levels.
    4. A deployment report must be written to /home/user/deployment/deployment_report.log with **exactly** the following 4 lines, each terminated by a single “\n” (newline) and nothing else:
       timestamp:2023-12-11T10:00:00Z
       backup_file:/home/user/backups/app_backup_pre_release.db
       logs_archive:/home/user/app/logs_archive_pre_release.tar.gz
       release_info_rows:1
       (Whitespace and capitalisation must match precisely.)

    OPERATIONS TO PERFORM
    A. Create the backup (Step 1 above).  
    B. Apply the migration file found at /home/user/update_scripts/02_create_release_info.sql to the live database (Step 2).  
       • After applying the migration, insert the single required row: version = “v1.4.0”.  
    C. Archive the logs directory into the specified gzip’d tarball (Step 3).  
    D. Write the deployment report (Step 4).

    CONCURRENCY REQUIREMENT
    • Steps A (database backup) and C (logs archiving) should be executed in parallel.  
      Use standard shell job control (“&” and “wait”) or any other POSIX-compliant mechanism so that both tasks run simultaneously before proceeding to the SQL migration and report generation.

    AUTOMATED TEST CHECKS
    The grader will verify:
    1. The backup file exists at the exact path given and is an SQLite 3 database **without** a “release_info” table.  
    2. The live database contains both tables (“users” and “release_info”) and exactly one row in “release_info” where version = “v1.4.0”.  
    3. /home/user/app/logs_archive_pre_release.tar.gz is a valid gzip-compressed tar file that contains every original “*.log” file and nothing else.  
    4. /home/user/deployment/deployment_report.log exists and its content matches the 4-line block shown above byte-for-byte.

    DO NOT delete, rename or move any of the existing files other than what is explicitly asked.  
    You may create any temporary working files in /home/user/tmp or /tmp, but they will not be checked by the grader.

    NOTE: You do NOT have root privileges; all commands must be executed as the “user” account.  
          You may assume that the “sqlite3”, “tar” and “gzip” utilities are installed and available in the PATH.

    When you are done, you can run ad-hoc commands such as “sqlite3 …” or “tar -tf …” to self-verify, but remember the automated test will use the exact criteria listed above.
